name: Potato Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions: Dissolve the yeast in warm water and mix all ingredients in a 1-gallon crock. Cover with a close-fitting lid; do not use a metal container. Let the starter rise until light (12 hours in warm weather, longer in cool weather). Do not let the starter get cold. After using part of the starter, add 1 cup warm water, 2 teaspoons sugar, and enough flour to mix back to the starter's original consistency. Add more potato occasionally as food for the yeast, but do not add more yeast. Use daily for best results; the starter improves with age.
